We had seen what hyper-hiring does to companies and wanted to avoid it at Linear. Our rule was at most, roughly double the team each year. 3 → 6 → 15 → 30 → 50 → 80 → 120 to maintain culture and stability. What followed was that we became profitable early, growth keeps accelerating, revenue per employee keeps rising, and attrition has stayed very low.

sources.name: Linear
sources.content:
# Linear
The product development system for teams and agents.
We're hiring: linear.app/careers
**Website:** https://linear.app | **Employees:** 230 | **Followers:** 79,701
**HQ:** San Francisco, California, US | **Founded:** 2019 | **Industries:** Software Development

## About
At Linear, we are on a mission to bring magic back to software. To empower product teams to do their best work, we are building an issue tracking and project management tool that combines UI elegance with world-class performance. Founded in 2019, Linear has become the tool of choice for thousands of high-impact companies to build their products better.

Linear was set up as a fully remote company from the start. Today, our small but mighty team is distributed across North America and Europe. What unites us is relentless focus, fast execution, and our passion for software craftsmanship. We are all makers at heart and care deeply about the quality of our work.

sources.name: Linear CEO Karri Saarinen: "Our customer base is quite powerful"
sources.content:
"We have this fairly simple idea that engineering is really the front line of all this information," Saarinen said. Tom Krazit May 15, 2025 · Linear CEO Karri Saarinen (Credit: Linear) May 15 2025 12:06 PM 7 min read Share ·
Follow LinkedIn RSS · Q&A · Linear started off as an issue-tracking tool helping developers coordinate on eliminating blockers and fixing problems, but has expanded into a product-development system. "We have this fairly simple idea that engineering is really the front line of all this information," Saarinen said. Tom Krazit May 15, 2025 · Linear CEO Karri Saarinen (Credit: Linear) May 15 2025 12:06 PM 7 min read Share ·
Linear has raised over $50 million to build a modern alternative, and it counts some of today's fastest-growing tech companies as customers. Founded in 2019 by CEO Karri Saarinen, Tuomas Artman, and Jori Lallo, Linear started off as an issue-tracking tool helping developers coordinate when eliminating blockers and fixing problems.
Today: An interview with Linear CEO Karri Saarinen on the role of AI in product-management software, CoreWeave's up and down year has an up and down week, and the latest enterprise moves.
Linear now has 14,000 customers using its software to build their own products — including OpenAI, Scale AI, and Brex — and unusually for an enterprise tech startup of its age, the company has been profitable since the beginning, Saarinen said.

sources.name: Inside Linear: Why craft and focus still win in product building | Karri Saarinen (Co-founder and CEO)
sources.content:
Karri Saarinen is the co-founder and CEO of Linear, the project management tool built for high-performance software teams. Since its founding in 2019, Linear has achieved a valuation of $1.25B and now counts companies like OpenAI, Ramp and Vercel ...
Karri Saarinen is the co-founder and CEO of Linear, the project management tool built for high-performance software teams. Since its founding in 2019, Linear has achieved a valuation of $400 million and now counts companies like OpenAI, Ramp and Vercel as customers.
And then I was at the Airbnb when there were like, already quite massive scale and, but they were still growing. and it was kinda like good to see like how different companies operate, how the CEOs operate. Like what, what goes wrong? Even in this like very successful companies. And I think you, realize that even like these very successful companies are, they have problems too. Like every company has problems. And, and as a startup you don't have to get everything right, like you just have to get few things right. And then you can like, fix things over time. Brett: you think linear would be successful if you didn't work at Coinbase and Airbnb?
And then maybe the third thing was that I noticed in all of the companies I worked at, that actually the, the ICS and the high level leadership was, I think more philosophically aligned. Because I think CEOs and a lot of the high level leadership, they just wanna see things happening.
I think like, few lessons I've learned, one was the, the business side that, like, I probably at the time, like after that company thought like if I start a new company, I will definitely start a company that I know there's a market for and make sure that there's a market so linear.

In our latest blog, Linear Co-founder Tuomas Artman explores what this concept looks like, as well as the early lessons learned while building Linear, including:
• Narrow down your initial audience and build for them: Figure out who you're building the product for and make the target audience as small as possible before expanding.
• Build and leverage your waitlist: The waitlist is the grinding stone with which you can sharpen your idea into something truly valuable that will succeed at market, so use it effectively.
• Trust your gut and validate demand with your users: Talk, talk, talk to your users and find out how invested in the product they are (and if they’d be willing to pay).  
Read more in our latest blog (link in comments).

sources.name: Maria 01 Builders' Breakfast: Rethinking the MVP – Lessons from Tuomas Artman, Linear – maria.io
sources.content:
# Maria 01 Builders’ Breakfast: Rethinking the MVP – Lessons from Tuomas Artman, Linear  
Hosted by:Maria 01  
Join us for the next edition of **Builders’ Breakfast**, where visionary founders share their real-life startup stories—the wins, the challenges, and the breakthroughs. Join us for a fireside chat with **Tuomas Artman**, Co-Founder of [Linear](https://linear.app/homepage), the modern issue-tracking tool that’s gained significant traction in the US tech scene. Backed by [Sequoia Capital](https://www.sequoiacap.com/article/linear-spotlight/) and [Accel VC](https://www.accel.com/), Linear has redefined how startups approach product development.  
**What to Expect:**  
- **Beyond the Traditional MVP:**Tuomas will share why the classic “just ship it” mentality doesn’t always cut it. Discover how Linear focused on building a product that was not just viable but*substantially better*than existing solutions.
- **Strategic User Engagement:**Learn how Linear utilized waitlists not just as a buzz generator but as a strategic tool to gather meaningful feedback and refine their product before a full-scale launch.
- **Designing with Purpose:**Understand how Linear’s Finnish roots influenced their commitment to craftsmanship, simplicity, and user-centric design, setting them apart in a crowded market.  
Whether you’re a founder, product manager, or developer, this session offers valuable insights into building products that resonate in today’s competitive landscape.
**Reserve your spot now and enjoy a morning of insightful conversation, networking, and, of course, breakfast.**  
Doors open & breakfast served  
Welcome words by Maria 01  
Fireside chat with Tuomas Artman  
Q&A  
Networking  
End of the event  
Can’t see the form? **You can access it here directly.**

sources.name: Explore 158 apps in Linear Integrations
sources.content:
Last updated 25 October 2025  
3.80% over the last 30 days.  
In Linear's ecosystem  
Founded in 2019, Linear is a project management and issue tracking tool to help software development. Co-founded by Karri Saarinen, Jori Lallo and Tuomas Artman who brought experience from companies like Airbnb, Coinbase and Uber.  
Based in San Francisco, Linear aims to simplify workflows for product teams by combining beautiful UI with high performance.Linear has features for project planning, issue tracking and team collaboration. Their design philosophy is simplicity and efficiency so teams can focus on shipping good software without the clutter of traditional project management tools.  
With a valuation of $400m and over $52m raised from Sequoia and Accel, Linear is a big player in the software development space. Linear achieved strong product market fit by initially targeting small tech startups and then expanding to bigger companies.  
(% of all Linear marketplace partners)  
Linear partners that are listed in 4 or more app marketplaces and have earned 25 or more reviews.

sources.content:
## Current Position **Community Events Manager**

- Led the planning and execution of Maria 01 NOW, a large-scale startup event with 100+ startups pitching across 2 stages, 40 jury members, and 500+ attendees.
- Curated and hosted ourBuilders' Breakfast series focused on founder stories, with speakers like Tuomas Artman (Linear), Niko Karstikko (Bob W), Kristo Ovaska (Taito.ai), and Sokari Saarenmaa (Aiven).
- Organise and host other key events like afterworks and founders' meetups that bring together startups, partners, and the wider tech ecosystem.
- Handle internal comms, onboarding, and Slack updates to keep members informed and involved.
- Act fast and adapt to what’s needed — content, feedback collection, comms, or logistics.

We are delighted to share a personnel announcement about Linear Capital: Jun Yang, former Co-founder and CTO of Dada, has recently joined Linear Capital as a Venture Partner. Jun will assist in deal sourcing, participate in project evaluations, and be an advisor of portfolio companies.
He has worked as an engineer and engineer manager at technology companies in Silicon Valley such as Google, Facebook, and Square. In 2015, he joined Dada (which went public in 2020) as a Co-founder and CTO. Jun received his undergraduate and master's degrees in Computer Science and Engineering from Zhejiang University, and later earned his Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University. Linear Capital's founding partner and CEO, Harry Wang, said: "I met Jun 15 years ago when we worked together at Facebook.

I switched gears (a while ago, in July actually 🙈) and moved to Linear from GitLab after more than 7 years.

sources.name: Learn how Linear handles privacy
sources.content:
#### Name  
Tuomas Artman
### Data Processing Agreement  
The DPA is a contract between you and Linear that has to be in place when processing personal data and transferring it to Linear.  
View Linear's Data Processing Agreement
### Subprocessors  
Subprocessors are companies that provide a service for Linear and who have or potentially will get access to personal data of Linear customers.  
View Linear's complete list of subprocessors
